pattern.description: Search pattern (regex by default, use --literal for exact text)

type.description: File type filter: go, py, ts, js, etc. (repeatable, use without dots)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal text, not regex

context.description: Show N lines of context around each match

tools.name: glob

type.description: Type filter: f=files only, d=directories only

tools.name: tree

tools.name: check

Great, I found the main structures in both files. Here's what they contain:

### participant_service.py (Python)
A service class for managing participants in a database-driven setup:

**Class: `ParticipantService`**
| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `get_participant(participant_id)` | Fetch a single participant |
| `create_participant(participant_data)` | Create new participant |
| `update_participant(participant_id, data)` | Update existing participant |
| `delete_participant(participant_id)` | Remove a participant |
| `list_participants(filters=None)` | List all participants (with optional filters) |

---

### participant_manager.js (JavaScript)
A client-side manager using an in-memory Map for tracking:

**Class: `ParticipantManager`**
- Uses a `Map()` to store participants (keyed by ID)
- `addParticipant(participant)` – Add new participant
- `removeParticipant(id)` – Remove by ID

---

**Key Observations:**
- The Python file (`participant_service.py`) appears to be for backend/database operations
- The JavaScript file (`participant_manager.js`) is for frontend/client-side tracking
- Both seem to track participants by a unique `participant_id` (e.g., "P-12345" format)

messages.content:
Here's what I found:

### program.go
Actually a very basic "Hello, World!" file – just a simple entry point with no actual program logic defined yet. (The earlier grep results for structs were likely from imports.)

---

### program_config.yaml
A configuration file for the arts program:

```yaml
version: "1.0"
name: "Arts Program Config"
description: "Configuration for arts program"
enabled: true
debug_mode: false

database:
  host: "localhost"
  port: 5432
  name: "arts_db"

api:
  base_url: "https://api.example.com"
  timeout: 30
  retry_count: 3
```

---

### programs/ directory
A more complete sub-project with its own structure:

```
programs/
├── src/
│   ├── main.go          # Entry point
│   ├── utils.go         # Utility functions
│   └── config/
│       └── settings.json
├── tests/
│   ├── integration_test.go
│   └── unit_test.go
├── docs/
│   ├── README.md
│   └── API.md
└── scripts/
    ├── build.sh
    └── deploy.sh
```

---

**How they connect:**
- The **config** points to a PostgreSQL database (`arts_db`) and an API endpoint
- The **programs/** directory looks like the main backend implementation with its own source, tests, docs, and deployment scripts
- The participant tracking (Python/JavaScript) would likely interact with this backend

Would you like me to look into the programs/src/main.go or the docs/API.md to understand more about how the program backend works and connects to participants?

messages.content:
Excellent! Now I have the complete picture. Here's what connects participants to programs and tracks their engagement:

---

## Data Model: How Participants Connect to Programs

### attendance/models.py (Django)
The key relationships that tie everything together:

```
Program
    ↓ (one-to-many)
Participant (ForeignKey to Program)
    ↓ (one-to-many)
Enrollment (ForeignKey to Participant)
    ↓ (one-to-many)
Attendance (ForeignKey to Enrollment)
```

---

## Functional Components

### student_enrollment.py
Manages enrollment operations:
| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `enroll_student(participant, program)` | Enroll a participant in a program |
| `register_for_program(participant, program)` | Register for a specific program |
| `drop_program(participant_id, program_id)` | Remove enrollment |
| `get_participant_details(participant_id)` | Get participant info |
| `list_participants()` | List all participants |

### attendance/attendance.py
Tracks daily attendance:
- `mark_present(student_id, date)` – Record present
- `mark_absent(student_id, date)` – Record absent
- `get_attendance(student_id, start_date, end_date)` – Get attendance history

### attendance/models.py (Attendance model)
Detailed attendance record with:
- `date` – Date of attendance
- `check_in` / `check_out` – Time tracking
- `status` – present/absent
- `duration` – Calculated time in program
- `is_late()` – Late arrival check

---

**Summary:** You have a complete enrollment and attendance system! The data flow is:
1. **Programs** are defined (with prerequisites from visual_arts_project/programs.yaml)
2. **Participants** enroll in programs via `StudentEnrollment`
3. **Enrollment** records link participants to specific programs
4. **Attendance** is tracked per enrollment with check-in/out times

Would you like me to look at any specific part in more detail, or explore the test files to see how this system is supposed to work?

This is exactly what I was looking for! Now I can see the full picture - we have the enrollment system that links participants to specific programs, and then attendance tracking that builds on top of that enrollment. The Django model with check-in/check-out times and status tracking is really helpful to understand how we'd monitor participation.

I think I have a good understanding now of how this codebase is organized for managing our social programs. Thanks for walking me through all of this!

[TASK_END]

You're welcome! I'm glad I could help you get oriented. Here's a quick summary of what we covered:

**Project Structure:**
- Frontend components (Button, Input, Modal) in `src/components/`
- Utilities and API helpers in `src/utils/`
- Test files in `tests/`

**Participant & Program Management:**
- **participant_service.py** (backend) and **participant_manager.js** (frontend) – participant tracking
- **visual_arts_project/** – program definitions with prerequisites, participant enrollment records
- **student_enrollment.py** – enrollment operations (enroll, drop, list)
- **attendance/** – Django models and system for tracking check-in/check-out, attendance status

**Key Data Flow:**
```
Program → Participant → Enrollment → Attendance
```

The codebase is a mix of stub files and functional modules – the participant tracking and attendance pieces have the most actual implementation.

Feel free to come back if you have more questions as you explore further. Good luck with your work on the social programs!

structured_turns.assistant_message:
Excellent! Now I have the complete picture. Here's what connects participants to programs and tracks their engagement:

---

## Data Model: How Participants Connect to Programs

### attendance/models.py (Django)
The key relationships that tie everything together:

```
Program
    ↓ (one-to-many)
Participant (ForeignKey to Program)
    ↓ (one-to-many)
Enrollment (ForeignKey to Participant)
    ↓ (one-to-many)
Attendance (ForeignKey to Enrollment)
```

---

## Functional Components

### student_enrollment.py
Manages enrollment operations:
| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `enroll_student(participant, program)` | Enroll a participant in a program |
| `register_for_program(participant, program)` | Register for a specific program |
| `drop_program(participant_id, program_id)` | Remove enrollment |
| `get_participant_details(participant_id)` | Get participant info |
| `list_participants()` | List all participants |

### attendance/attendance.py
Tracks daily attendance:
- `mark_present(student_id, date)` – Record present
- `mark_absent(student_id, date)` – Record absent
- `get_attendance(student_id, start_date, end_date)` – Get attendance history

### attendance/models.py (Attendance model)
Detailed attendance record with:
- `date` – Date of attendance
- `check_in` / `check_out` – Time tracking
- `status` – present/absent
- `duration` – Calculated time in program
- `is_late()` – Late arrival check

---

**Summary:** You have a complete enrollment and attendance system! The data flow is:
1. **Programs** are defined (with prerequisites from visual_arts_project/programs.yaml)
2. **Participants** enroll in programs via `StudentEnrollment`
3. **Enrollment** records link participants to specific programs
4. **Attendance** is tracked per enrollment with check-in/out times

Would you like me to look at any specific part in more detail, or explore the test files to see how this system is supposed to work?
